asset_alt: Archived ASCII-art safety benchmark diagram for vision-in-text adversarial prompting
asset_caption: Archive diagram: ASCII-art / vision-in-text benchmark folded into Human CAPTCHA attack references.
title: Archive: ASCII-Art Sensitive-Content Benchmark
description: Archive reason: ArtPrompt / ViTC already covers the ASCII-art jailbreak benchmark, and this topic is outside the embodied-AI portfolio loop.
summary: The ASCII-art safety idea is archived as a completed external benchmark direction rather than a Survey project. It remains useful background for adversarial prompt formats and Human CAPTCHA attack design.

details.text: ArtPrompt introduces the Vision-in-Text Challenge and an ASCII-art jailbreak attack against aligned LLMs, which covers the core benchmark idea. The dashboard should not duplicate it as a new project.

details: Moved-out live work: if needed, Human CAPTCHA can borrow this as one adversarial encoding family, but it is not an embodied-AI project on its own.
Archive decision: keep historical reference only.
risks_decisions: Reason type: already completed by a paper/benchmark and outside current embodied portfolio scope.
Reopen condition: only if the Human CAPTCHA benchmark explicitly adopts visual-in-text process signals as a core measurement axis.
Notion migration 2026-05-31: the open checkbox about LLM ASCII-art sensitive-content detection is intentionally archived as a safety-benchmark side project outside the embodied robotics loop.
